{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/7334","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/7334/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/7334/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/7334/events","html_url":"https://github.com/napari/napari/issues/7334","id":2591649424,"node_id":"I_kwDOCJ0aI86aeW6Q","number":7334,"title":"Closing the Viewer calls `dims` `current_step` event","user":{"login":"paulshuker","id":46575643,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ2NTc1NjQz","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/46575643?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/paulshuker","html_url":"https://github.com/paulshuker","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/paulshuker/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/paulshuker/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/paulshuker/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/paulshuker/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/paulshuker/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/paulshuker/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/paulshuker/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/paulshuker/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/paulshuker/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":1022379511,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMDIyMzc5NTEx","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/labels/bug","name":"bug","color":"d73a4a","default":true,"description":"Something isn't working"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-10-16T11:54:06Z","updated_at":"2024-10-16T11:54:06Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### 🐛 Bug Report

Dimension current step event is called twice during the Viewer `close` function.

### 💡 Steps to Reproduce

1. `pip install napari==0.5.4`
2. Run:
```py
import napari
import numpy as np


class Bug:
    def __init__(self):
        self.viewer = napari.Viewer()
        rng = np.random.RandomState(0)
        img = rng.rand(4, 30, 50)
        self.viewer.add_image(img)
        self.viewer.dims.events.current_step.connect(self.changed_z_plane)
        print(f"Closing Viewer")
        self.viewer.close()

    def changed_z_plane(self) -> None:
        print(f"Z plane changed")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    Bug()
```

### 💡 Expected Behavior

No z plane change event calls during Viewer closing?

### 🌎 Environment

napari: 0.5.4
Platform: Windows-10-10.0.22631-SP0
python: 3.11.10

### 💡 Additional Context

I do not know if this is intended, feel free to close this if you do not consider it a bug.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/7334/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/7334/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

<!-- A clear and concise description of the feature proposal -->
Add possibility to hide technical layers from layer list in the interface

## Motivation

When creating advanced widgets which add some information to canvas (like for 3D activity from today's "napari euro dev sync" meeting) the layer list is polluted with multiple layers which should not be controlled by the user, and also make the interface less readable and make harder to access to 

<!-- Please outline the motivation for the proposal. Is your feature request related to a problem? e.g., I'm always frustrated when [...]. If this is related to another GitHub issue, please link here too -->

## Pitch

Add method to mark layer as technical/hidden to not show it in the interface. Possible options:

*  add a property like hidden, technical which default value will be false:
* layers which names start from `.` be hidden (like Unix directories)

These layers should still be accessible from `viewer.layers`. Maybe a good idea to add to the settings option to make hidden layers visible. 

<!-- A clear and concise description of what you want to happen. -->

## Alternatives

There was a proposition to use layers groups when it comes to the napari codebase, but this did not solve problem when one would like to mix order of technical and data layers. 

<!-- A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered, if any. -->

## Additional context

<!-- Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here. -->
